title: Managed Database on Render (Free PostgreSQL)
description: Managed PostgreSQL on Render with point-in-time recovery on paid tiers and straightforward Git-integrated deployment. Free tier imposes a 90-day database expiration and lacks automated backups — suitable for prototypes only; upgrade to a paid tier for production use.

cost.costNotes: Free tier: PostgreSQL (90-day expiration), Redis (25MB). Production use requires Starter plan ($7/month for PostgreSQL).

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: This pattern explicitly uses Render's free tier managed database for prototypes/MVPs. Min $0 represents staying within free tier limits (PostgreSQL with 90-day expiration, 25MB Redis). Max $0 reflects that this pattern is specifically designed for free-tier usage. Once requirements exceed free tier (production workloads, persistent databases beyond 90 days), users would transition to Render's paid plans ($7/month PostgreSQL Starter) or adopt a different pattern.
provenance.adoptionCost: Low adoption cost due to Render's simple database provisioning model requiring minimal configuration. Developers need 4-8 hours to: understand free tier limitations (90-day DB expiration, no automated backups), configure database connection strings, and test database connectivity. With AI coding assistants providing Render configuration templates, implementation time is reduced.
provenance.licenseCost: Render is a commercial PaaS with a generous free tier that includes PostgreSQL and Redis at $0 cost. No licensing fees or upfront costs required to use the platform. Free tier is available indefinitely for qualifying usage (within resource limits and time constraints like 90-day database expiration). Paid plans are usage-based with no license purchase required.

provides.reasoning: Render provides managed PostgreSQL with 90-day expiration on free tier. Suitable for prototypes; paid tiers remove the expiration and add point-in-time recovery.
Render provides managed Redis with 25MB storage on free tier, suitable for caching or session management in small-scale applications.
Render-hosted PostgreSQL is standard Postgres; native RLS via CREATE POLICY is fully supported.
Render managed PostgreSQL runs standard Postgres with no proxy limitations, supporting PREPARE TRANSACTION for XA/2PC distributed transactions.

requires.reasoning: Render deploys from Git repositories (GitHub/GitLab). Source code must be in version control to use this hosting pattern.

supports_nfr.reason: Free tier managed database lacks redundancy and has 90-day expiration, limiting availability guarantees to 95%.
Render free PostgreSQL has no automated backups; RPO is effectively 24 hours (1440 minutes). Manual backups required for any data recovery.
Minimum RTO of 15 seconds (0.25 minutes) required for database instance startup.
Free tier database compute constraints support maximum ~1 job/second or 3600 jobs/hour ceiling.
Free tier is optimized for low-traffic prototypes/MVPs. At >50 QPS, consider paid plans for better performance.
Free tier shared PostgreSQL constraints limit write query throughput to 50 QPS ceiling.
All tenant isolation strategies are supported through PostgreSQL and application-level implementation.
PII handling in free tier may lack compliance agreements, requiring careful evaluation.
GDPR compliance may require paid plans with formal compliance agreements beyond free tier capabilities.
HIPAA compliance typically requires paid plans with BAAs not available on free tier.
CCPA compliance may require paid plans with formal compliance agreements beyond free tier capabilities.
SOX compliance typically requires paid plans with audit trails and compliance certifications.
PostgreSQL on Render supports read-your-writes consistency for single-instance deployments.
Free tier PostgreSQL has 90-day expiration policy and no automated backups, making strict long-term durability guarantees impossible.

supports_constraints.reason: Render is a cloud-agnostic PaaS that abstracts underlying infrastructure provider details.
Render managed database supports all major backend languages through standard database drivers.
Render managed database can serve as the data backend for any platform type.
Pattern specifically requires Render as the database provider
Render provides free Redis (25MB) for optional caching though capacity is limited.
Redis can be used for basic message queues within 25MB limit for optional async messaging.
Multi-tenancy is supported at small scale on free tier with PostgreSQL row-level security.
Single PostgreSQL instance supports transactions but distributed coordination requires external tooling.
Real-time streaming can be implemented through Redis pub/sub within free tier constraints.
PostgreSQL with pgvector extension can support vector search though performance may be limited on free tier.
PostgreSQL JSONB columns can serve as document store for optional document-oriented data models.
Free Redis (25MB) provides key-value store capability for optional fast lookups.
Graph queries can be implemented in PostgreSQL though native graph databases are not provided.
PostgreSQL can handle time-series data though specialized time-series extensions may be limited on free tier.
Free PostgreSQL supports OLTP workloads within 90-day expiration and capacity constraints.
Free-tier database has limited storage and compute unsuitable for analytical OLAP workloads.

database_enabled.description: Enable free PostgreSQL database (90-day expiration, no automated backups). Suitable for prototypes but not production.

redis_enabled.description: Enable free Redis instance (25MB limit). Useful for caching, sessions, or job queues in MVP stage.

ssl_mode.description: Render PostgreSQL enforces SSL. Use 'verify-full' for production workloads requiring certificate validation.
